title: Children Of A Lesser Bog

jokes.text: Aw. He's using his jacket as some kind of security blanket. That's so cute and pathetic.
jokes.setup: Character observing Fry's attachment to his jacket
jokes.punchline: The contradiction of 'cute and pathetic' delivers gentle mockery
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor with nice contradiction in the punchline

jokes.text: Oh, hey, guys. I'm just sucking on my jacket. A Clump-O-Honey candy got stuck right there over a thousand years ago, and I can still taste it.
jokes.setup: Fry casually admits to sucking on his jacket
jokes.punchline: The absurd revelation about thousand-year-old candy still being tasty
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry behavior - perfectly integrated character comedy with absurd sci-fi premise

jokes.text: Zoidberg, you residue hog! Now all I taste is crab juice.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg presumably licks or contaminates the jacket candy spot
jokes.punchline: 'Residue hog' and the disgusting result of tasting crab juice
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific insult with disgusting but logical consequence

jokes.text: I can't. They stopped making it after it got reclassified as industrial glue.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to buy more candy
jokes.punchline: The candy was so sticky it became classified as industrial glue
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist premise with perfect economy - the industrial glue classification is a great escalation

jokes.text: Don't forget, I came in third on MasterChef Junior.
jokes.setup: Bender offers to recreate the candy recipe
jokes.punchline: His cooking credential is third place on a children's cooking show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender boastfulness undercut by the absurd credential - third place on a kids' show

jokes.text: Sure. You seem like the kind of guy who has a mouth.
jokes.setup: Character asks to taste Bender's candy creation
jokes.punchline: Bender's bizarre assessment that the person 'seems like' they have a mouth
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist deadpan - treating having a mouth as an uncertain characteristic shows perfect robotic logic

jokes.text: Whoa! Diabetic coma! / Hm. I better add some sugar to make it less sweet.
jokes.setup: Character goes into diabetic coma from the candy's sweetness
jokes.punchline: Bender's illogical solution to add sugar to make it less sweet
jokes.explanation: Good robot logic failure - the backwards solution to sweetness is perfectly Bender

jokes.text: And just a touch of treacle to remind me of my dear departed Aunt Juanita. She drowned in treacle.
jokes.setup: Bender adds treacle for sentimental reasons about his aunt
jokes.punchline: The dark revelation that she drowned in the very ingredient he's adding
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy misdirection - starts sentimental, ends morbid in a perfectly twisted way

jokes.text: You know I'm dying on the floor.
jokes.setup: Bender continues cooking while someone is in diabetic coma
jokes.punchline: The understated complaint about literally dying
jokes.explanation: Solid reaction beat with good deadpan delivery but fairly standard structure

jokes.text: It's a little sticky. It's a lot sticky! And there's something hard in it.
jokes.setup: Character tastes the candy and reports it's sticky
jokes.punchline: Quick escalation from 'little' to 'lot' sticky, plus mysterious hard object
jokes.explanation: Basic escalation comedy that sets up the next joke but isn't particularly inventive

jokes.text: Luckily, I'm an expert at fixing human beak bones. Are you free Friday for an expensive appointment?
jokes.setup: Someone presumably broke their jaw on the candy
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's incompetent medical offer calling them 'beak bones' and admitting it's expensive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg - incompetent medicine with alien terminology and blatant money-grabbing

jokes.text: Shmeird. There's some old alert I set up 20 years ago. Our babies?!
jokes.setup: Amy checking calendar for appointment, finds old alert
jokes.punchline: The shocked realization about forgotten babies after 20 years
jokes.explanation: Good plot-driving comedy with Amy's characteristic forgetfulness about major life events

jokes.text: Something to do with the meatbag life cycle?
jokes.setup: Discussion about babies and reproduction
jokes.punchline: Bender's dismissive robot terminology for human biology
jokes.explanation: Standard Bender dehumanization but perfectly in character

jokes.text: I have video of the birth. Wanna see it? No! That is the wrong kind of delivery!
jokes.setup: Amy offers to show birth video
jokes.punchline: Bender's pun on 'delivery' meaning both birth and mail/package delivery
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay pun but fairly predictable setup and payoff

jokes.text: Life is disgusting. / No, it was beautiful. But I was so not prepared to be a mother.
jokes.setup: Bender declares life disgusting after seeing birth video
jokes.punchline: Amy's contradiction followed by honest admission of unpreparedness
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Bender's disgust and Amy's conflicted feelings

jokes.text: Then can I have your car? No! Too late. I already crashed it.
jokes.setup: Bender asks for Amy's car during emotional moment
jokes.punchline: Reveals he already crashed it before getting permission
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender behavior - opportunistic request revealing he's already committed the crime

jokes.text: 'Cause we're so rich, that's like a penny.
jokes.setup: Parents say they wouldn't miss birth for a million dollars
jokes.punchline: Explanation that a million dollars is meaningless due to extreme wealth
jokes.explanation: Standard rich parents joke but fits their established characters well

jokes.text: I know. I recognize your head.
jokes.setup: Kif introduces himself formally
jokes.punchline: Grand Midwife's blunt, obvious observation about recognizing his distinctive head
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response that deflates Kif's formal introduction

jokes.text: and not for any optional cash donation people sometimes place in that basket. It's green, and there's a little sign on it. Found it!
jokes.setup: Grand Midwife claims not to do ceremony for donations
jokes.punchline: Immediately describes donation basket in detail, Amy instantly finds it
jokes.explanation: Good ironic corruption - claiming not to want donations while giving detailed directions to donation basket

jokes.text: Or not. It's unpredictable. Guess I'll have to wing it. Anybody here from out of town?
jokes.setup: Grand Midwife announces ceremony will commence
jokes.punchline: Admits it's unpredictable and resorts to generic standup comedy crowd work
jokes.explanation: Clever meta humor about ceremonial authority figures resorting to comedy club filler

jokes.text: Oh my. I'm so anxious, my camouflage reflex is kicking in. / No, it's helpful. I wanna look cute for our hundreds of kids.
jokes.setup: Kif becomes anxious and camouflages
jokes.punchline: Amy's misunderstanding that it's helpful for looking cute rather than hiding
jokes.explanation: Good alien biology comedy with Amy's characteristic misunderstanding of the situation

jokes.text: What's that mean? / It means don't start naming them yet.
jokes.setup: Amy asks what 'the Winnowing' means
jokes.punchline: Ominous implication that many babies won't survive to need names
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with perfect economy - the implication is crystal clear and disturbing

jokes.text: Four of them made it! Well, three and a half.
jokes.setup: Counting surviving tadpoles after the winnowing
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of a half-surviving tadpole
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with the casual mention of partial survival

jokes.text: Ew. She's touching them.
jokes.setup: Amy presumably touches her newborn children
jokes.punchline: Leela's disgusted reaction to normal maternal behavior
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction comedy about the grossness of alien reproduction

jokes.text: Eh... Well, in-in the sense that all children are beautiful.
jokes.setup: Grand Midwife declares children beautiful
jokes.punchline: Awkward backtrack to generic platitude suggesting they're actually ugly
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy from authority figure trying to be diplomatic about ugly babies

jokes.text: They can call me Captain Uncle. / Uh, I'll go dispose of this medical waste. With a little butter and lemon.
jokes.setup: Brannigan offers uncle title, Zoidberg mentions disposing medical waste
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg plans to eat the medical waste with seasoning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg behavior - disgusting but matter-of-fact about eating medical waste

jokes.text: Want a picture with the kids? No need. The way they look is burned into my mind.
jokes.setup: Offer to take family photo
jokes.punchline: Bender's traumatized response suggesting the image is unforgettable (but not in a good way)
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sounds sentimental until you realize it's traumatic

jokes.text: They're nice, you know? Just a kinda little... Super gross.
jokes.setup: Amy tries to diplomatically describe the children
jokes.punchline: Leela bluntly finishes with 'super gross'
jokes.explanation: Standard completion joke where one character's diplomacy is undercut by another's bluntness

jokes.text: That'll be our little secret.
jokes.setup: Finally being grandparents
jokes.punchline: Treating grandparenthood as something shameful to hide
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy about shallow parents embarrassed by ugly grandchildren

jokes.text: They're the sweetest thing on the menu. Not even close!
jokes.setup: Elzar calls the children the sweetest thing on the menu
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction suggesting other menu items are much sweeter
jokes.explanation: Decent subversion of expected compliment with restaurant context making it ambiguous

jokes.text: Question mark.
jokes.setup: Character asks about babies being different sizes
jokes.punchline: Literally saying 'question mark' instead of using questioning inflection
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor - verbalizing punctuation is a classic sci-fi comedy trope done well

jokes.text: Mama! Wrong again. / He's shrively! You haven't seen the half of it.
jokes.setup: Baby calls Amy mama, she corrects him, character comments on appearance
jokes.punchline: Amy's ominous suggestion that the visible shriveliness is only partial
jokes.explanation: Standard parenting comedy with hint at worse physical problems

jokes.text: Do you always have to talk about me?
jokes.setup: Amy mentions Axl is like his father
jokes.punchline: Baby's precocious complaint about being discussed
jokes.explanation: Good precocious child humor - baby already displaying adult-like self-consciousness

jokes.text: Seriously? Oh, my God. I must have been super drunk. / Leela, you bone brain! You didn't have sex. Bone.
jokes.setup: Leela thinks she had drunken sex with Kif
jokes.punchline: Amy's correction and Bender's emphasis on 'bone' as both insult and sexual reference
jokes.explanation: Standard misunderstanding comedy with mild wordplay from Bender

jokes.text: Bone.
jokes.setup: Leela called a bone brain
jokes.punchline: Bender just says 'bone' appreciatively
jokes.explanation: Simple Bender sexual innuendo response

jokes.text: And it's filthy. / Oh, yeah! Educate me, baby.
jokes.setup: Educational movie about alien reproduction described as filthy
jokes.punchline: Bender's excited sexual response to 'educational' content
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response - turning educational content into sexual excitement

jokes.text: Except the Amphibiosans. They just barely touch hands while the rest of us are gettin' it on. Yowza. Eh, that was pretty hot.
jokes.setup: Narrator explains Amphibiosans have boring reproduction compared to others
jokes.punchline: Bender finds even the boring hand-touching arousing
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Bender's perverted nature finding arousal in mundane contact

jokes.text: Eh, that was pretty hot.
jokes.setup: Boring reproduction method shown
jokes.punchline: Bender finds it arousing anyway
jokes.explanation: Bender's perverted perspective on innocent act

jokes.text: Are you sure I wasn't drunk? I-I mean, I'm drunk now.
jokes.setup: Leela questioning if she was drunk during reproduction
jokes.punchline: Casual admission she's currently drunk
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection revealing current state while questioning past state

jokes.text: I'm afraid there's no time to discuss the many presents I bought, some of which are large.
jokes.setup: Brannigan claims urgency prevents discussing gifts
jokes.punchline: Still manages to boast about the gifts while claiming no time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Brannigan - claims no time to brag while actively bragging, specific detail about size

jokes.text: 784 West 28th Street? Uh-huh. Weird, th-that's what I got. Oh, Earth! Ah! Coulda swore you said Kepler 10B.
jokes.setup: Babysitter confused about address
jokes.punchline: Was on wrong planet entirely - went to distant star system
jokes.explanation: Good sci-fi comedy escalation - simple mix-up becomes interplanetary error

jokes.text: Look at me, chugging mineral water on a weeknight! So wild and free!
jokes.setup: Amy describing her evening activities
jokes.punchline: Treating drinking water as wild rebellious behavior
jokes.explanation: Good parental exhaustion comedy - mineral water as peak excitement shows how limited her life has become

jokes.text: They're a nightmare. A-A sweet, adorable nightmare.
jokes.setup: Leela calling the children a nightmare
jokes.punchline: Quick correction to maintain politeness while keeping the insult
jokes.explanation: Standard diplomatic backtrack comedy - honest assessment quickly sugar-coated

jokes.text: Bring a bowl of these candies to my quarters when you're done crying.
jokes.setup: Zapp empathizes with Kif
jokes.punchline: Immediately makes selfish demand
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp - brief sympathy followed by selfishness

jokes.text: If there's nothing further, I will... / Oh no, you willn't.
jokes.setup: Kif trying to leave politely
jokes.punchline: Brannigan's made-up contraction 'willn't' to keep him there
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay - 'willn't' is a ridiculous but logical contraction that sounds perfectly Brannigan

jokes.text: These delicious Clumps aren't for eating. Mm! Mm... They're for manscaping. Manscape me, Kif. That's an order.
jokes.setup: Brannigan eating the candy while claiming it's not for eating
jokes.punchline: Reveals disgusting plan to use sticky candy for body hair removal, orders Kif to do it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Brannigan grossness - inappropriate use of food, inappropriate orders to subordinate, callback to episode's MacGuffin

jokes.text: It looks like the whole planet has psoriasis. / You're looking at the back of my head, sir.
jokes.setup: Brannigan making dramatic observation about planet's condition
jokes.punchline: Kif's matter-of-fact correction that he's looking the wrong direction
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of Brannigan's dramatic moment - perfect setup/punchline with great character dynamic

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Transmitter got ripped up by bears. That's what we study here. Bears. I'm a bear biologist.
jokes.setup: Casual explanation about transmitter damage
jokes.punchline: Deadpan introduction of studying the same bears that caused the damage
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan humor about professional studying destructive subjects

jokes.text: I used to be a whale biologist, but I hate whales. Hate them. / Well, then it's good you found an animal you like. / Nope. Still looking.
jokes.setup: Bear biologist's hatred of whales, Brannigan assumes he likes bears
jokes.punchline: Reveals he hates bears too and is still searching for an animal to like
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - misanthropic biologist who hates all his subjects, good escalation

jokes.text: This bear species is known as tardigrades. It's a fancy word for ugly. Oh, shut up, you stupid bear!
jokes.setup: Scientific explanation of bear species name
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong etymology and direct insult to the bears
jokes.explanation: Great character consistency - fake scientific authority combined with unprofessional animal hatred

jokes.text: Are you bat crackers? If they smell human food, they go bat crackers!
jokes.setup: Brannigan about to eat candy near the bears
jokes.punchline: Bear biologist's warning using 'bat crackers' as both question and description
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with the repeated phrase serving double duty as insult and warning

jokes.text: Let's go! Faster! Faster! Go, horseface, go! Neigh!
jokes.setup: Children riding Leela like a horse
jokes.punchline: Leela's defeated horse sound showing complete submission
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy with Leela's degradation, the 'neigh' shows complete defeat

jokes.text: Rum raisin tastes just like caterpillars!
jokes.setup: Child commenting on ice cream flavor
jokes.punchline: Disturbing comparison suggesting they've eaten caterpillars
jokes.explanation: Good alien child humor - casual mention of eating insects as reference point

jokes.text: Are you done spoiling my kids rotten so they'll love you more?
jokes.setup: Amy confronts Leela
jokes.punchline: Accusation of manipulation through kindness
jokes.explanation: Good jealous parent behavior

jokes.text: Well, you can trot back to the barn now!
jokes.setup: Amy dismissing Leela angrily
jokes.punchline: Continuing the horse metaphor to insult Leela
jokes.explanation: Decent callback extending the horse metaphor for Amy's anger

jokes.text: Stop licking my velour! It's dry clean only!
jokes.setup: Presumably alien creatures attacking Brannigan
jokes.punchline: His concern about clothing care during life-threatening situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Brannigan priorities - vanity about clothing during monster attack, very specific dry cleaning concern

jokes.text: And stay away from my scrotums!
jokes.setup: Brannigan under attack by creatures
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately specific anatomical concern during combat
jokes.explanation: Typical Brannigan inappropriate concern, though less inventive than the dry cleaning line

jokes.text: plus trace DNA from a certain Scruffy. Scruffy's stuff gets around.
jokes.setup: Scientific explanation of genetic contributors
jokes.punchline: Scruffy's matter-of-fact acknowledgment of his genetic promiscuity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy deadpan - treating genetic contamination as casual fact about himself

jokes.text: Yeah, FYI, y-you've got a little jelly right there.
jokes.setup: Serious legal proceeding concluding
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Leela's jelly stain from earlier confession
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier visual gag, shows attention to detail

jokes.text: Help me, Kif! They found the secret candy in my girdle!
jokes.setup: Brannigan in danger from alien creatures
jokes.punchline: Reveals he hides candy in undergarments, and that's what attracted them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Brannigan grossness - secret candy stash in girdle, callback to episode's MacGuffin

jokes.text: Naked Kif, you saved my life! You have my undying loyalty. Eat Kif! He's boneless!
jokes.setup: Brannigan expressing gratitude to Kif for saving him
jokes.punchline: Immediately offers Kif to the monsters as food, with helpful preparation tip
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Brannigan betrayal - instant reversal from gratitude to sacrifice, with helpful culinary advice. Peak character comedy.

jokes.text: Okay, but it doesn't lessen the shame.
jokes.setup: Kif arrives for the challenge
jokes.punchline: Grand Midwife's acknowledgment that his presence doesn't fix the shameful situation
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan authority figure humor - maintaining moral judgment despite resolution

jokes.text: Bear number 23 was also stupid, fighting for scraps while I dined on salmon tartare. None for you, losers!
jokes.setup: Bear biologist describing battle with bears
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was eating fancy food while bears fought for scraps, then taunts them
jokes.explanation: Great character consistency - bear biologist's contempt for subjects extends to taunting them during combat

jokes.text: Some call it a tiki hut.
jokes.setup: Grand description of 'brutal confines of the Inquisidome'
jokes.punchline: Casual admission it's just a tiki hut
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic buildup - 'brutal Inquisidome' is just a casual tiki hut

jokes.text: Oops! That wasn't supposed to happen. Cheap, lousy wall.
jokes.setup: Presumably wall collapses during ceremony
jokes.punchline: Grand Midwife's casual complaint about construction quality
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy callback to the cheap construction implied by 'tiki hut'

jokes.text: For only a small additional fee.
jokes.setup: Allowing children to hug parents after ceremony
jokes.punchline: Monetizing even basic family affection
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier corruption theme - charging for family reunification

tmdb_overview: Amy and Kif's children emerge from an alien swamp.
